checks.finding: REQ-001..REQ-006 each specify concrete files, schemas, functions, or grep-verifiable behavior (e.g., REQ-001: loop-inventory.json validates against loop-inventory/v1 with cross-checked driver_scripts and authorization-pair mapping; REQ-006: CHANGELOG/validate-repository/skill-reference-sync conformance). Every requirement is paired with a corresponding AC row in acceptance-tests.md carrying a concrete pass/fail assertion (AC-001..AC-018), so downstream task authors and verifiers have an observable target for each requirement.

acceptance-tests.md's Requirement column maps every goal to at least one AC: REQ-001->AC-001-004,017; REQ-002->AC-005-007,017; REQ-003->AC-008-010,017; REQ-004->AC-011-013,017,018; REQ-005->AC-004,008,013,014,015; REQ-006->AC-016. No REQ-00N in requirements.md's Goals section is left without a matching AC.

Each AC row in acceptance-tests.md specifies an externally observable outcome (schema validation, grep match, artifact presence, exit/verdict state) plus, for AC-001, AC-002, AC-007, AC-010, AC-012, AC-017, AC-018, an explicit negative self-check describing the mutation that must turn the check red. None rely on vague intent language.

requirements.md's Non-goals section explicitly excludes re-fixing #143 (fix already at HEAD), modifying any protected-gate file/precheck/validator/guard/hook config or protected test, A5-A7 and Pillar B issues (#145-#148) by number, building the #125 harness itself, and tasks.md/traceability.md as Phase 2 artifacts -- bounding the areas where this test-infrastructure feature could otherwise expand.

The Security Boundaries table (B1-B3) states auth/authz requirements, PII/data classification (synthetic-fixtures-only, internal-source-only), and regulatory constraints for each trust boundary. Roles and Permissions states agent/human/CI boundaries including human sign-off ownership of OQ-5. The Assumptions section states stability preconditions (2d8c6a5 ancestry, #112 script landed at HEAD, protected-gate-table exclusion of run-all.sh/.ps1/test.yml).

The Risks section lists Critical (cap-drift, fixture/gate-semantics divergence), High (CI runtime cost), and Medium (RED-differential history dependency, brownfield-before-seed) risks, each with a mitigation tied to a concrete AC (AC-001, AC-002, AC-007, AC-010, AC-012, AC-017) or a documented evidence procedure (RED-differential recording), giving every high-risk claim a planned validation surface.
